rounds_summary.attempted_route: RandomForestRegressor (200 trees) + ECFP4-1024 drug features + AAC+dipeptide-420 protein features + -log10(Kd+1e-6) label transform; train split only
rounds_summary.observation: exit_code=0, data_correctness=True, func_correctness=False; scores were differentiated but drug ranking was incorrect vs. reference

rounds_summary.attempted_route: PyTorch MLP (1024->512->256->1) + MorganGenerator ECFP + AAC+ConjointTriad-363 protein + pKd=9-log10(Kd) + train+val combined; per-feature z-norm on full concatenated vector
rounds_summary.observation: All 77 antiviral drugs received identical pKd=7.8007; per-feature z-norm of sparse binary ECFP bits collapsed drug variance to zero

rounds_summary.attempted_route: PyTorch MLP (512->256->128, BatchNorm+Dropout) + AllChem ECFP bits kept raw (no norm) + only protein block z-normalized + 50 epochs + StepLR scheduler
rounds_summary.observation: Predicted pKd=4.4930 for all 77 drugs (range=0.0000); model still collapses when all inputs share identical OOD target embedding

ruled_out_routes: RandomForestRegressor + ECFP4-1024 + AAC+dipeptide-420 protein features: produces non-degenerate but functionally incorrect ranking
PyTorch MLP with per-feature z-score normalization applied to full concatenated ECFP+protein vector: collapses binary drug features to zero variance

PyTorch MLP (any depth) + raw ECFP + z-normalized ConjointTriad-363 protein + single OOD target at inference: model output degenerates to constant when drug-target pair shares identical target embedding
ECFP4 (1024-bit) concatenated with global protein descriptors (AAC+CT) as the sole drug discriminator against one novel COVID target: insufficient inter-drug signal when target is fixed and OOD
synthesised_diagnosis: All three rounds fail because the MLP (and RF) learn a joint drug-target embedding that relies on target variation to separate drugs; at inference every antiviral drug is paired with the exact same COVID-19 3CL protease embedding (an OOD kinase-domain-absent sequence), so drug-side ECFP variation cannot propagate through the trained weights to produce different outputs. Round 1's non-degenerate scores were RF extrapolation noise, not signal. Rounds 2 and 3 made the collapse explicit. No amount of normalization tuning fixes a model that has not learned to discriminate drugs against a single held-out target type.
rationale: The DAVIS-trained joint drug-target model cannot discriminate drugs against a single fixed OOD target; drug-side ECFP variance is only recoverable if the model sees drug variation paired with multiple targets during training and inference, not a single identical OOD sequence.
recommended_alternative: Use a dual-encoder Siamese architecture: train separate drug encoder (ECFP -> MLP) and target encoder (ESM-2 or ProtTrans pretrained embedding, frozen, then linear projection) with a bilinear or dot-product interaction head. At inference, drug embeddings will differ because they pass through the drug-specific encoder without target conditioning. Use torch.hub.load('facebookresearch/esm', 'esm2_t6_8M_UR50D') for target encoding and sklearn.metrics.pairwise.cosine_similarity to validate inter-drug score spread before ranking.
